name: El Castell de Xativa -- Trail run circular
name_original: El Castell de Xàtiva — Trail run circular
region_intro: Jogging around Xàtiva offers a diverse landscape characterized by a dramatic mountainous backdrop and the historic Xàtiva Castle perched on a ridge. The region features varied terrain, including pine forests, the narrow valley of the Albaida River, and historical paths. These physical features provide a range of running experiences, from challenging ascents to more gentle riverside trails. The area's natural beauty and historical sites are well-suited for outdoor activity. Best jogging routes around Xàtiva The most popular jogging route is El Castell de Xativa -- Trail run circular, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail that takes about 1 hour to complete. This route features significant elevation gain as it circles the historic Xàtiva Castle. Another top favourite among local runners is The Ice House loop from Xàtiva, a difficult 10.6 miles (17.1 km) path. This trail offers extensive views across the mountainous landscape with substantial elevation changes. Local runners also love the Les Arcades old bridge – Arcadetes d’Alboi Aqueduct loop from Xàtiva, a 9.1 miles (14.7 km) trail leading through the Albaida River valley and past historical aqueducts, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Jogging around Xàtiva is defined by mountainous terrain, extensive pine forests, and historical paths. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to challenging climbs. The routes in Xàtiva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 24 reviews. More than 1700 runners have used komoot to explore Xàtiva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Xàtiva?
A: There are over 50 running routes around Xàtiva, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include paths with significant elevation changes, riverside trails, and routes through pine forests.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes for beginners in Xàtiva?
A: Yes, while many routes in Xàtiva feature challenging terrain, there are a few easier paths suitable for beginners. The region's varied landscape ensures options for different fitness levels, including more gentle trails along the Albaida River valley.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Xàtiva?
A: Jogging routes in Xàtiva are characterized by a dramatic mountainous backdrop, extensive pine forests, and historical paths. You'll find varied terrain, from challenging ascents with panoramic views to more gentle paths along the Albaida River valley, often featuring Mediterranean vegetation.

Q: Can I find long-distance running trails in the Xàtiva area?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a longer challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31465340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Ice House loop from Xàtiva&lt;/a&gt; offer substantial distances, covering over 10 miles (17 km) with significant elevation changes and extensive views across the mountainous landscape.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by historical landmarks or points of interest?
A: Many routes in Xàtiva incorporate historical landmarks. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e832570653/el-castell-de-xativa-trail-run-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Castell de Xativa -- Trail run circular&lt;/a&gt; takes you around the iconic Xàtiva Castle. Other trails, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6901773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Arcades old bridge – Arcadetes d’Alboi Aqueduct loop from Xàtiva&lt;/a&gt;, lead past historical aqueducts like Les Arcades d'Alboi, adding a cultural dimension to your run. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/653158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova Negra&lt;/a&gt; archaeological site along the Albaida River.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Xàtiva?
A: The running routes in Xàtiva are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 24 reviews. Over 1700 runners have used komoot to explore the area, often praising the varied terrain, historical sites, and beautiful natural environment.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Xàtiva?
A: Yes, several circular running routes are available. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e832570653/el-castell-de-xativa-trail-run-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Castell de Xativa -- Trail run circular&lt;/a&gt;, which loops around the historic Xàtiva Castle. Another is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31526822" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sun Tower of Xàtiva – Castell de Xàtiva loop from Xàtiva&lt;/a&gt;, offering a moderate challenge.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Xàtiva?
A: Xàtiva's Mediterranean climate makes it suitable for jogging for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended during those months. Winters are mild, providing good conditions for outdoor activity.

Q: Are there any family-friendly jogging paths in Xàtiva?
A: While many routes involve elevation, the area around the Albaida River valley, particularly near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/653158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova Negra&lt;/a&gt;, offers relatively flat and enjoyable paths that can be suitable for families looking for a less strenuous run or walk.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails in Xàtiva?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many of the natural trails around Xàtiva, especially in less populated areas and pine forests. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash and check for specific local regulations, particularly in protected natural areas or near historical sites.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. Xàtiva is set against a mountainous landscape, and many routes provide sweeping views. The ascent to Xàtiva Castle itself offers impressive vistas. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31465340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Ice House loop from Xàtiva&lt;/a&gt; also provide extensive views across the mountainous terrain, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/362227" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Creu del Cardenal&lt;/a&gt; summit is another great viewpoint.

Q: How can I access the jogging routes in Xàtiva, especially regarding parking or public transport?
A: Many routes around Xàtiva are accessible from the city center, which can be reached by public transport. For routes further afield, parking is often available near trailheads, particularly for popular spots like the Xàtiva Castle or areas leading into the natural parks. It's recommended to check specific route details for parking information.
tips: Old well (18th century) to collect snow that would later be turned into ice to trade or use as a preservative. It is about 10m in diameter and the same in depth. It also has beautiful views towards the city of Xàtiva.
A very cool and entertaining path along the sunny side with very nice views of the castle and its fortress. It is a very pleasant route with a surface in very good condition.
